✕

    About me

    Big ideas, real builds, and a refusal to do things the boring way

    I'm Aaditya Bhave. I'm a first year at Penn State, and I've been taking things apart and building better ones for as long as I can remember. If something looks impossible, or just badly done, that's usually the exact thing I want to make.

    Where it started

    I’ve been obsessed with robotics since I was about three years old. Blame the movies I grew up on: Enthiran, Real Steel, Robocop, and a whole lineup of others where machines think and move and matter. That feeling never wore off, and honestly everything I build is really just me chasing it.

    What I'm into

    I’ve lived and studied across the UK, the US, Canada and India, so I’ve picked up a little bit of everywhere along the way. When I’m not building hardware I’m usually developing Roblox games, playing lawn tennis, buried in a book (any genre, I’m not picky), or working with music going the whole time. Lately that’s a lot of Anirudh and Tame Impala. I like doing things the unconventional way, because the normal way is rarely the interesting part.

    How my brain actually works

    ⚡ Wired for the hard stuff

    • Reasoning, math, and taking problems apart is where I come alive.
    • Routine, repetitive work bores me, and I'll cheerfully coast through it.
    • But hand me a genuine challenge and I lock in completely, the kind of focus where hours just vanish. I do my best work when I'm inspired, not when I'm told.

    ⌘ I direct the AI, not the other way around

    • I love building with AI, but I never let it drive.
    • I sketch the skeleton first, the architecture and the plan, worked out in my notebooks before a single line gets written.
    • Then I bring AI in to help fill it in while I stay in the director's seat, reviewing and steering every step. The thinking stays mine.

    Curious by default. Bored by easy. Always building something.

    🤖 Curious what I've built? Have a look around the site, or come say hi on GitHub.
